metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5064
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2: Subcellular fractionation patterns of Clmp protein in the rat brain showed Clmp is enriched in synaptic, but not PSD, fractions. In HEK293T cells, co-immunoprecipitation of Clmp and PSD-95 (DLG4) showed these proteins do not form a complex (Clmp relatives like IgSF11 are known to interact with PSD-95). Clmp does not have a functional PDZ-binding motif.

Figure 3: "Clmp deletion leads to changes in AMPAR, KAR, and NMDAR mEPSCs in Clmp-/- CA3 neurons; increased frequency and amplitude of AMPAR mEPSCs, increased frequency, amplitude, and decay time constant of KAR mEPSCs, and decreased frequency of NMDAR mEPSCs."

Figure 5: Extracellular and cytoplasmic regions of Clmp interact with AMPARs and KARs.

Figure 6: "However, we detected no genotype difference in LTP at MF-CA3 synapses (Figures 6H,I) or post-tetanic potentiation (PTP) (Figure 6J) of excitatory synaptic transmission at MF-CA3 synapses, as validated by the inhibition of synaptic transmission by the group II mGluR agonist DCG-IV (Figure 6K) and high paired-pulse ratios (Figure 6L). Together, these results suggest that Clmp does not affect presynaptic function or synaptic plasticity at MF-CA3 synapses."

Figure 7: Increased extrasynaptic KAR currents and decreased extrasynaptic NMDAR currents in Clmp-/- hippocampal CA3 neurons.. Experimental description: M&M: "The Clmp-/- mice used in the present study were originally generated by Genentech, Inc. (Tang et al., 2010)."

antibody:
"Peptides containing mouse Clmp (aa 345-373) were used to immunize guinea pigs (2090). The specificity of anti-Clmp antibodies (2090) was confirmed by immunoblot experiments using Clmp-/- whole-brain lysates."
